title: spilanthes acmella var. oleracea
intro: Spilanthes acmella var. oleracea, also known as toothache plant, is a plant species within the Acanthaceae family, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds, including spilanthol and acmella acid. The medicinal properties of Spilanthes acmella var. oleracea have been used to treat various health conditions, including toothaches, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health complaints.
